terms.definition: A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function to choose a slot, giving average O(1) operations.
A function that maps a key to an integer; for hash tables it must be fast and distribute keys evenly.
One entry of the hash table's backing array; can hold one element or a chain of colliding elements.
Two distinct keys hashing to the same slot, requiring chaining or probing to resolve.
A collision-resolution strategy where each slot stores a list/linked list of colliding entries.
A collision-resolution strategy where collisions probe a sequence of alternative slots until a match or empty slot is found; CPython's strategy.
The ratio of stored entries to bucket count (n / m); crossing a threshold (≈ 0.66 in CPython) triggers a resize.
Allocating a larger backing array and recomputing each entry's slot in the new table — an O(n) event amortized over many inserts.
An object that has `__hash__` returning a stable integer consistent with `__eq__`; required for use as a key.
